notes.sourceCoverageGap: Rows missing from one source while present in the other are tracked as coverage gaps, not winner conflicts. The atlas keeps the primary-source row and marks the secondary gap separately.
notes.turnout: Seat-level turnout differences under the actionable threshold are tracked as source variance, not launch blockers.
notes.turnoutElectorateBase: Large turnout deltas with near-matching votes polled but materially different elector totals are tracked as electorate-base variance between sources, not ballot-count conflicts.
notes.turnoutSourceVariance: Turnout deltas above the headline threshold but with the same electorate base and only moderate total-vote drift are tracked as source variance, not manual-review blockers.
notes.voteShare: Winner vote-share differences under the actionable threshold are tracked as source variance, not launch blockers.
notes.voteShareDenominator: Large winner vote-share deltas with near-matching winner votes but materially different total-vote denominators are tracked as denominator variance, not winner-vote conflicts.
notes.voteShareFormula: Winner vote-share deltas with matching winner-vote and total-vote counts are treated as source percentage-calculation variance, not manual-review blockers.

findings.rationale: Published 2014 Andhra Pradesh result references align on Pulaparty Narayana Murty of TDP for Gannavaram, while the LokDhaba-aligned staged overlap appears to have crossed into a different winner sheet. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

Published 2014 Andhra Pradesh constituency result pages align on Varupula Subbarao of YSRCP for Prathipadu, while the LokDhaba-aligned staged overlap appears to be mis-keyed to a different winner sheet. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

The staged IndiaVotes Karimganj 2019 seat row is internally broken: the winner label is blank and the winner vote share collapses to 0.3 despite the total-vote denominator matching the LokDhaba row. The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba result sheet.

The staged IndiaVotes single-seat Dadra & Nagar Haveli rows show a recurring stale-candidate contamination across multiple cycles, reusing Delkar Mohanbhai Sanjibhai even when party, winner-vote, and winner-share fields diverge sharply from the LokDhaba row. The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba result sheet.

The staged IndiaVotes single-seat Dadra & Nagar Haveli rows show a recurring stale-candidate contamination across multiple cycles, reusing or misaligning the seat-level winner sheet when winner-vote and winner-share fields diverge from the LokDhaba row. The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba result sheet.

The staged IndiaVotes single-seat Dadra & Nagar Haveli rows show a recurring stale-candidate contamination across multiple cycles, reusing or misaligning the seat-level winner sheet when winner-vote and winner-share fields diverge from the LokDhaba row. The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba result sheet.

The staged IndiaVotes Delhi 2019 Lok Sabha file carries corrupted winner and runner-up candidate labels while preserving the correct BJP/INC vote totals and margins. The LokDhaba row matches the published Delhi 2019 result surface for this seat, so the atlas resolves to the LokDhaba winner sheet.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Aurangabad 2009 sums to 731,147 votes, which matches the LokDhaba row exactly and not the lower IndiaVotes summary-row total of 598,332. The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba totals.

Both sources agree on the winner and vote totals, but IndiaVotes collapses the historical faction label to a generic RPI code while LokDhaba preserves the RPG tag used in the staged overlap set. The atlas resolves the seat to the LokDhaba-aligned historical party label.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Latur Rural 2019 sums to 172,099 votes, matching the IndiaVotes row exactly and not the higher LokDhaba total of 199,599. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Ghatkopar West 2009 sums to 142,950 votes, matching the IndiaVotes row and not the lower LokDhaba total of 112,590. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

IndiaVotes captured the initially misdeclared result. Contemporary Nagaland reporting says the result was reviewed and corrected in favour of NDPP candidate Namri Nchang by 168 votes, which matches the LokDhaba row.
Multiple public result summaries align with the LokDhaba row for Tobu 2018, while the captured IndiaVotes detail appears stale and under-counted for both total votes and candidate totals.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Pfutsero 2013 sums to 16,548 votes, matching the IndiaVotes row and not the higher LokDhaba total of 20,589. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Pfutsero 2008 sums to 18,185 votes, matching the IndiaVotes row and not the higher LokDhaba total of 20,969. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Pallahara 2009 sums to 86,699 votes, matching the IndiaVotes row and not the higher LokDhaba total of 107,957. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

The staged LokDhaba overlap for Embalam 2016 is internally inconsistent, collapsing turnout and total-vote volume far below the published constituency result. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row, which aligns with published Puducherry 2016 winner lists.

Both sources agree on P. Rajavelu as winner, but the LokDhaba overlap collapses the total-vote base to 13,428 while keeping the winner tally at 12,933, producing an implausible 96.31% winner share. The atlas resolves the seat to the IndiaVotes row for turnout and vote-share integrity.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Alwar Rural 2008 sums to 98,059 votes, matching the IndiaVotes row and not the lower LokDhaba total of 79,761. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

The LokDhaba overlap for Udaipur 2008 is internally broken: the winner tally and total-vote count both lose an order of magnitude, dropping Gulab Chand Kataria from 65,706 votes to 6,570 and turnout from 57.5% to 7.88%. The atlas resolves the seat to the IndiaVotes row.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Maharajganj 2009 sums to 839,206 votes, which matches the LokDhaba total exactly and not the lower IndiaVotes summary-row total of 599,531. The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba totals.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Bishnupur 2011 sums to 154,406 votes, matching the LokDhaba row and not the higher IndiaVotes summary-row total of 177,890. The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba totals.
